results.name: Stylized 3D Printable Industrial Chemical Plant
results.description:
Description: A versatile, low-poly stylized Chemical Plant model featuring industrial smokestacks, reinforced silos, and glowing green vats. This asset is specifically designed for multi-purpose use, ranging from 3D printing for tabletop gaming to digital environment dioramas.

The structure is built on a solid foundation base, making it ideal for physical printing without needing complex supports for the main body. Its distinct silhouette and "toxic" green accents make it a perfect fit for sci-fi, post-apocalyptic, or modern industrial settings.

Optimized for 3D Printing: Sturdy geometry suitable for resin or FDM printing.

Design: Stylized industrial aesthetic with glowing windows and chemical containers.

Applications: Perfect for Warhammer 40k terrain, Star Wars Legion, D&D dioramas, or game engine background assets.

Features: Multiple levels, rooftop pipes, and a integrated baseplate for easy placement in scenes.

results.name: Hard Surface Mega Kit Volume 02
results.description:
I am presenting a Hard Surface Mega Kit, a comprehensive collection of elements and IMM Brushes sculpted to assist artists in hard surface modeling and design across multiple fields. This pack serves as a versatile production toolkit for building robots, vehicles, characters, and mechanical assets, as well as armors, protective suits, clothing elements, and accessories featuring hard surface aesthetics.

The collection includes a broad variety of modular components suitable for shoulder pads, elbow guards, knee protection, chest plates, and mask construction, enabling artists to develop complete designs from small details to full sci-fi exosuits. Its flexibility makes it possible to sculpt and concept characters or robots in styles inspired by Cyberpunk 2077, Star Wars, Alien, Star Trek, and Warhammer, while maintaining high visual quality and anatomical balance.

results.name: Molly Yolkeree
results.description:
This is one of my long-term projects that I decided to extend for my own learning purposes—not just in character sculpting, topology, texturing, and concepting, but also to explore animation in Blender. What better way to learn than by bringing my own original character to life?

I started animating around December 2024, but I spent time beforehand polishing the rig and building pose libraries between September and November 2024. After months of studying and self-teaching in my free time, learning 3D animation has become a valuable experience I can keep under my belt—especially when it comes to conveying stylized, expressive, and human originality in my work.

This project has given me more confidence as I move forward to my next goal: creating my own indie RPG in Unreal Engine 5. Of course, I’ll be mindful of the scope, but I’m excited to shape it into something fun and tangible—especially since I really love storytelling.

results.name: SIMPLE_CITY PACK (LOWPOLY)
results.description:
Simple City – Low-Poly 3D City Pack (URP Supported)
Simple City is a low-poly asset pack designed to help you quickly create a vibrant urban environment. Ideal for games or animations, this pack includes everything you need to build a functional city, optimized for performance.
What’s Included:
Buildings: School, Hospital, Bank, Library, Museum, Supermarket, Police Station, Fire Station, Hotel, Office, Post Office.
Shops: Pizza Shop, Burger Shop, Coffee Shop.
Vehicles: Cars, Buses, Bus Stops.
Urban Details: Street Lights, Traffic Lights.
Features:
URP Support: Fully compatible with Unity’s Universal Render Pipeline.
Modular Design: Easily customize and expand your city.
Optimized for Performance: Low-poly models that perform well on all platforms.
Start building your city today with Simple City—a fast and easy way to bring urban environments to life in Unity!


🔗 Get this model on the Unity Asset Store Search for “SIMPLE_CITY PACK (LOWPOLY)” on Unity Asset Store by DHILRUBA

results.name: Nicholas Costelloe Headstone
results.description: Slate broken headstone in St. Flannan’s Graveyard (CL045-033018-), Killaloe, Co. Clare, marking the grave of Nicholas Costelloe. Above the inscription there is an I H S Christogram with six-pointed star rising from the upturned crossbar of the H with heart beneath, all of which is contained within a beaded roundel.  Christogram is flanked on either side by soul-effigies beneath encircled six-pointed rosette also known as a hexafoil/daisy wheel. Traces of broken motif above the six-pointed rosettes. A hexafoil, also known as a daisy wheel or rosette, is a geometric symbol with six lobes, often resembling a six-petal flower. It's commonly found as an apotropaic mark (a symbol believed to ward off evil) and is frequently seen on historical buildings, gravestones, and other structures. The six-petal rosette is common in 17th to 20th century folk art throughout Europe. According to Wikipedia Tthe hexafoil design is modeled after the six petal lily, for its symbolism of purity and relation to the Trinity.

results.name: Tatooine - 3D Modeling Final Project
results.description:
This diorama is inspired by the desert planet Tatooine from the Star Wars franchise. It shows a starfighter and repair shop. The animation features a technician checking or fixing the starfighter's damaged engine.

Objects
1. Tatooine-style building (with a tent, antenna, and satellite dish)
2. X-Wing Starfighter
3. Moisture vaporator
4. Crate
5. Barrel
6. Toolbox
7. Ladder

Character 
Technician

Techniques used:
1. Modeling
2. UV unwrapping with marked seams
3. Texturing using textures from website e.g textures.com, manual texture painting in Blender, and Substance Painter. The maps I used include base color, normal, roughness, and metallic maps.
4. Animating using rig, rigify and keyframe

*Note: For the moisture vaporator, I created the mesh in Blender, exported it to Substance Painter for texturing, and then **export it back into Blender** for final touch.*

results.name: Star Wars The Lars Homestead
results.description:
Had to make this one. Not sure of all the details surrounding the igloo. Looked at a ton of material from the ANH, but couldn’t figure out all of it. The prequels looked a bit different, as well. The entrance is about 2m high. OK bg filler.

The sand-mesh can easily be removed, so you can sink the building into your own environment if needed.

The rest of the place (the two maintenance/garage bays) will be built at a later time if time allows.

results.name: Mos Espa - StarWars Tunisia Film location
results.description:
Created in RealityCapture by Capturing Reality from 32 images in 00h:00m:23s.

Mos Espa Star Wars, port spatial de la planète Tatooine dans la saga de science-fiction de ”Star Wars” du réalisateur George Lucas,

Le site de Mos Espa a été construit à Ong Jmel, près de Nefta, dans le sud-ouest tunisien, au début des années ’90, pour servir de décor principal au tournage de ce qui allait devenir une des sagas les plus célèbres des superproductions de l’industrie cinématographique américaine.

Vous voulez revivre la grandeur nature des scènes des films de Star Wars, alors ce circuit est pour vous!

Tataouine et les ksour environnants ont fortement inspiré George Lucas pour ses films.

Même le nom de la ville, Tataouine a servi d’inspiration pour le nom de la planète désertique de Star Wars, la planète Tatooine.

Le tournage des films s’est réparti sur différents lieux du sud de la Tunisie.

results.name: First Time Bust
results.description:
We all have to start somewhere. I had a blast maknig my first bust. I have a long way to go before I am a pro at this. There are so much tiny details to a bust I realized I forgot a few of them, as well as my eyelash UV's refusing to work for this project no matter what I did, so I had to continue into texturing. Doing this shown me that her eyelashes are going to take form of the entire texture pallet, so if you zoom in, you can see the terrifying UV layout lining her lashes. 

Outisde of that, doing the hair was a challenge. Next project I would like to learn how to make long hair, as well as the eyelash error not being a problem. The ears and lips were my favorite parts. Now that I have my issues out in the light, I can begin building an even better bust next time around! We all have to begin somewhere.

results.name: Sci Fi Greeble Package 1
results.description:
For many years I've been building models from the Star Wars universe, as well as from World War 2 and uploading them to Sketchfab.

Going back to how the ships in Star Wars were first created, they used plastic models that were kitbashed together into the familiar forms we know and love today.

I reuse the parts created over and over, but I also add new parts to the collection as time goes on.

Here is a package with 382 such greebles. The greebles themself are not very complex, because I wanted to retain the look and feel of the plastic models. 

The greebles are not textured, because they should be part of a larger model and be textured together with it.

results.name: A Minimalist Airport Diorama
results.description:
A minimalist airport diorama designed for your game or cinematic scene.

This set includes a baked airport terminal building, three detailed aircraft models — Boeing 777 (JAL), Airbus A380 (SIA), and a smaller Airbus A318 — along with jetways and various airport vehicles.

All parts are modular, except for the main building, making it easy to customize and adapt to your project needs.

Completely free for personal and commercial use. To edit the scene, simply clear all parent objects first, then delete the empty groups — this is the easiest way to modify any of my models.

Download now and start building your own airport scene. If you like this model, please leave a like and follow my profile for more free, high-quality 3D environments and detailed dioramas.

results.name: Wigancice Żytawskie (Weigsdorf) - Dom Agaty
results.description:
Wigancice Żytawskie (germ. Weigsdorf) is a village situated in Turoszów valley, south-western Poland. It's origins date back to XIV century, when inhabitants from Prussia started to settle down in this area and unique wattle and daub houses were built all along the village. After the World War II, Turoszów valley was assigned to Poland and people from different polish regions were moved here in order to take over old german buildings. Despite being strangers to each other, polish inhabitants managed to create a close community in this remote but beautiful area. However, in 1999 the vilage was liquidated due to Turów lignite mine expansion. Currently it's slowly falling into oblivion...

Model presents the last homestead preserved to modern times, called Agata's House.

results.name: Voxel tank constructor
results.description:
Итак это старая версия моего конструктора воксельных танков. Он предназначен для создания ваших собственных танков. В конструкторе имееться несколько корпусов, башен, пушек(есть 1 автопушка) и катков. Для начала создания воксельной бронетехники самый отличный вариант.
So this is the old version of my voxel tank builder. It is designed for creating your own tanks. The designer has several buildings, turrets, cannons (there is 1 autocannon) and rollers. To start creating voxel armored vehicles, this is the best option.

Если вы сделали модель с помощью этого конструктора и выложили её укажите меня как автора компонентов. Если вы хотите продать созданную вами модель то обратитесь ко мне: Телеграм- @Carmine_GOW4 , instagram- imask1074 , discord- danil_gow4 . 
If you made a model using this constructor and posted it, please indicate me as the author of the components. If you want to sell the model you created, then contact me: Telegram- @Carmine_GOW4, instagram- imask1074, discord- danil_gow4 .
